text: The surgeon performs a two-port laparoscopic appendectomy, inserting a 10mm optical trocar using the modified Scandinavian technique through an incision over the inferior umbilical crease. The tract is dilated with artery forceps, followed by the insertion of a 5mm working port in the suprapubic region. The acutely inflamed appendix is visible, and both the right and left ovaries appear normal upon examination.

The surgeon identifies the insertion point for the Veress needle near the right anterior superior iliac spine for optimal manipulation of the appendix. The needle is inserted, and the stylet is removed. A loop is introduced using a loop passer and grasped with a LigaSure Maryland dissector. The surgeon holds the tip of the appendix with the Maryland dissector, threads it through the loop, and tightens the loop externally through the needle.

The surgeon begins coagulating the mesoappendix from the tip towards the base of the appendix, identifying the appendicular artery and observing its pulsations. The artery is doubly coagulated and cut distally. The appendix is kept elongated during mesoappendix coagulation to facilitate removal. The loop is adjusted to hold the appendix, allowing further dissection towards its base. The appendix is pulled away from the cecum to stretch it, making the mesoappendix more visible for coagulation.

The surgeon completes the removal of the mesoappendix, ensuring to doubly coagulate any doubtful tissue before cutting distally. The appendix is fully excised, and the surgeon discusses different methods to ligate the base of the appendix.

The surgeon introduces a thread across the base of the appendix and ties an extracorporeal Mishiraj knot, using a Windarker knot pusher to tighten the loop. The appendix turns blue after ligation of the appendicular artery, indicating successful ligation. The thread is cut with scissors, and the appendix is severed two to three centimeters distally from the tight base. A fecolith attached to the appendix is visible and is removed using suction.
The surgeon gently removes the appendix through the five mm working port. Following the removal, the reactionary fluid from appendicitis in the pelvis is suctioned out, completing the procedure.
